name: Roasted Potato, Beet and Onion Salad
recipeIngredient: Sprigs of Fresh Rosemary or Thyme
3/4 Pound Baby Red or Chioggia Beets (stemmed and scrubbed)
recipeInstructions:
1. Halve or quarter potatoes lengthwise.
2. Toss the potatoes and garlic cloves with 2 tablespoons olive oil and herb sprigs. Season liberally with salt and pepper.
3. Arrange in a single layer on a baking pan, cover with foil, and set aside.
4. Toss onions and beets separately with remaining oil and season with salt and pepper.
5. Wrap onions and beets separately in foil.
6. Roast onions, beets, and potatoes in a preheated 375°F oven for 35-40 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
7. Uncover potatoes after 20 minutes to allow them to brown and crisp slightly.
8. Unwrap beets and onions carefully, reserving any juices for Balsamic Vinaigrette. Slip skins off beets if desired.
9. Arrange roasted vegetables attractively on plates and drizzle with Balsamic Vinaigrette.
10. Place a small mound of Herb Salad in the center and garnish with baby green beans, peas, and pea tendrils if available.
11. Serve warm or at room temperature.
Yield: 6 servings
Balsamic Vinaigrette:
- Juice from roasted beets and onions
1 1/2 tablespoons balsamic vinegar
3 tablespoons olive oil
Salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
2 teaspoons chopped parsley and/or chives
Whisk ingredients together. Store refrigerated.
Herb Salad:
1 cup loosely packed mixture of tender herbs, such as parsley, chervil, tarragon, burnet, chives, dill, along with any available flavorful flowers such as nasturtium, chive, or calendula.
